info.title: intraktible Decision API
info.description: The public integration contract for intraktible: execute decision flows, read decision history, manage flows as code, and drive the deployment lifecycle (deploy / promote) for CI/CD. Other administrative endpoints (RBAC, audit, secrets) are out of scope of this document. Authenticate every request with an API key in the X-Api-Key header.

tags.name: flows
tags.description: List, create, read, and import flows.

tags.name: deployments
tags.description: Deploy and promote versions across environments (CI/CD).

post.summary: Execute a flow
post.description: Runs the live version of the flow (champion, or A/B challenger) in the given environment against the supplied data and records the decision.

200.description: The decision was recorded (a flow whose logic errors is a recorded 'failed' decision, not an HTTP error).

post.summary: Batch-execute a flow over a dataset
post.description: Runs each row of a dataset through the recorded decide path (capped at 500 rows).

post.summary: Stream-execute a flow over a large NDJSON dataset
post.description: The large-job batch path: the request body is NDJSON (one input object per line) and the response is NDJSON streamed one result per line, flushed as each row decides. No dataset is held in memory and there is no row cap, so it scales to very large jobs. entity_type / entity_key are query parameters applied to every row. Each row is a real recorded decision (history, metrics, audit), like /decide/batch.

parameters.name: entity_type
parameters.description: Entity type recorded for every row.

parameters.name: entity_key
parameters.description: Input field each row's entity id is read from.

200.description: NDJSON: one decision result per line, streamed.

get.summary: Generated OpenAPI contract for one flow
get.description: Returns a self-contained OpenAPI 3.1 document describing this flow's decide and decide/batch endpoints, with the flow's latest published input schema as the request data schema. Point codegen / Swagger UI at it for a flow-specific contract.

post.summary: Define (or redefine) a predictive model
post.description: Registers a named model evaluated deterministically by the engine and referenced from a Predict node. spec.kind is logistic | gbm | expression | external: logistic {intercept,coefficients:{feature:weight}} · gbm {base?,link?:logit,trees:[{feature,threshold,left,right}|{leaf:true,value}]} · expression {expr:<expr-lang over features>} · external {endpoint:<https url>,timeout_ms?} (BYO served model — the shell POSTs the features and reads a {score,probability} response, egress-guarded).

get.description: Returns the model's rolling distribution of predicted probabilities (deciles) and, once a baseline is captured, the PSI vs that baseline (<0.1 stable, 0.1–0.25 moderate, >0.25 significant).

200.description: Drift report: count, decile histogram, and PSI when a baseline exists.

post.summary: Capture the current prediction distribution as the drift baseline
Explain a flow graph in plain language
post.description: Authoring copilot (backed by the configured AI provider). Returns a plain-language explanation of the posted {graph}. 503 if no provider is configured.

post.summary: Suggest decision logic from a natural-language requirement

parameters.name: start_time
parameters.description: Only decisions started at or after this RFC3339 time (alias: since).

parameters.name: end_time
parameters.description: Only decisions started at or before this RFC3339 time (alias: until).

parameters.name: include_node_results
parameters.description: Set false to omit each decision's per-node trace for a lighter response (the trace is always available on the decision-detail endpoint).

200.description: A page of decisions, newest first.
The decision record with its node-level trace.
All flows for the caller's tenant.

post.summary: Create a flow

200.description: The flow with its versions and deployments.

post.summary: Import a flow as code
post.description: Upserts a flow from an exported document: creates it when the slug is new, otherwise publishes the graph as a new version. Re-importing identical content is a no-op (200, published=false).

200.description: No change (the live latest version already matches).

201.description: A flow and/or version was written.

post.summary: Import many flows at once
post.description: Imports a bundle of flow-as-code documents (a GitOps repo). Best-effort: a failing flow is reported in its result rather than aborting the rest.

200.description: A per-flow report with rollup counts.

post.summary: Deploy a version to an environment
post.description: Makes a published version live in an environment (a direct deploy). Requires the approver role.

201.description: The version is live in the environment.

post.summary: Promote a live version up the environment chain
post.description: Ships the live version of `from` up to `to`. A non-production target deploys directly; production opens a maker-checker request. A promotion gate (firing monitors / failing assertions) can block it unless `force` is allowed for the stage. Requires the approver role.

200.description: Promoted directly, or a maker-checker request was opened (pending).
The resolved identity, scope, and role.

post.summary: Publish a new flow version (the primary authoring write). Node positions are optional — omit them and the server fills a deterministic swimlane auto-layout.

get.summary: Live decision metrics for a flow.
Get a flow's stored assertion cases.

post.summary: Run a flow's assertions against a version.
Replay a dataset through a published version (no recorded decision).
Sweep one input field across a range and report disposition transitions.
Define a monitor rule.
Evaluate monitors now and push the firing set to webhooks.
Snapshot the disposition mix as the drift baseline.

get.summary: Per-bucket distribution drift vs the baseline.
Get the per-environment shadow version + comparison report.

put.summary: Set the shadow version for an environment.

post.summary: Open a maker-checker deployment request (production).
Approve a deployment request (a different user).
Reject a deployment request.
Promote a population into pre-approvals via the decide path.
Create a policy bound to a flow.
Publish a policy version (disposition bands).
Backtest a policy over a dataset (records nothing).
Grant a pre-approval.

get.summary: List the case queue (filter by status/type/assignee).

post.summary: Open a review case.

get.summary: Get one case with its notes + audit log.

post.summary: Define an agent.
Run an agent synchronously.
Register a notification webhook.

data.description: The decision input, validated against the version's input schema.

entity_type.description: Optional Context Layer entity type; its features are injected under 'features'.

disposition.description: The policy's automated outcome, when a policy is bound.

challenger_pct.description: Percent of traffic routed to the challenger.

from.description: Source environment whose live version is promoted.

force.description: Override the promotion gate where the stage allows it.

promoted.description: True when the version deployed directly.

pending.description: True when a maker-checker request was opened (production).

scope.description: Environment the key may call: "sandbox", "production", or "*" (any). A scoped key is rejected (403) on a non-matching environment's decide endpoints.

NodePosition.description: Saved canvas coordinate (presentation only; the runtime ignores it). Omit it and the server fills a deterministic auto-layout.

config.description: Opaque, node-type-specific configuration; the API stores it verbatim and each node engine validates it at decide time. Shapes by type: input {} · assignment {assignments:[{target,expr}]} · rule {rules:[{when,then:[{target,expr}]}]} · split {condition:<bool expr>} (routes to the "yes"/"no" branch edges) · scorecard {factors:[{when,weight}]} · decision_table {hit:first|unique|any|rule_order|collect, aggregate?:sum|min|max|count (collect only), rows:[{when,outputs:[{target,expr}]}]} · 2d_matrix {rows,cols,cells} · reason {reasons:[{when,code,description}]} · manual_review {company_name,case_type,sla_days} (expressions) · code {source:<Starlark>} · connect {connector:<name>} · ai {agent:<name>,prompt,output} · predict {model:<name>,output} (injects predict.<output> {score,probability}) · output {fields:[...]}. expr/when/condition are expr-lang expressions over the decision input; the code node runs Starlark (see docs/EXPRESSIONS.md).

branch.description: Label for a conditional edge (e.g. "yes"/"no" out of a split); empty for unconditional flow.

Graph.description: A flow version's node/edge structure. Must have exactly one input node and at least one output node, unique node ids, and be acyclic; edges must reference existing distinct nodes (no self-loops).

etag.description: SHA-256 of {graph, input_schema}; an identical re-publish is a detectable no-op.

input_schema.description: JSON Schema for the decide input (optional).
JSON Schema for the decide input (optional).

AssertionsDoc.description: Stored input→expected test cases for a flow.

MonitorRule.description: A threshold rule over a flow's live metrics.

PromotionStagePolicy.description: Gates a promotion into one environment.

PolicySpec.description: A policy version: disposition bands + default.

PreApprovalGrant.description: A durable, time-boxed pre-decision for one entity.

fields.description: Case-insensitive field names masked at the read boundary.
